title: Generated hostnames sometimes exceed `HOST_NAME_MAX`
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

**NOTE**: I am opening this issue here rather than on the tracker for Buildah, as I can only replicate this with `podman build` (version 4.0.1, commit c8b9a2e) and not on `buildah build` (version 1.25.0, commit cdcaaf51); as such, I suspect this might be an issue with the currently vendored version of Buildah that has since been fixed upstream.

Container builds (via, say, `podman build`) happen in ephemeral containers which will, in some cases, receive a hostname composed of a random 64-character alphanumeric prefix, plus a static suffic (`working-container`), which exceeds the default value for `HOST_NAME_MAX` on Linux (64 characters) and causes issues with some software (Postfix, for one).

Strangely enough, this is not reproducible if pulling the hostname once, but doing so multiple times returns the incorrect value consistently and regardless of the underlying image used in `FROM`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a `Containerfile` that shows the working FQDN hostname for the ephemeral container, e.g.:
```
FROM docker.io/debian:bullseye-slim
RUN hostname --fqdn
RUN hostname --fqdn
```

2. Run `podman build`, see an invalid hostname show up, e.g.:
```
STEP 1/3: FROM docker.io/debian:bullseye-slim
STEP 2/3: RUN hostname --fqdn
debian-working-container
--> f094a0d04de
STEP 3/3: RUN hostname --fqdn
f094a0d04ded49d198aabe8fd440a7681d446a64c3e3faf99d5f78f002ec9942-working-container
COMMIT
--> 81c07c53d06
81c07c53d061536a1b60c5dd263955e829ba24d5f5e51fd2612cd9141d6dc789
```

3. There is no step 3.

**Describe the results you received:**

Ephemeral containers are being run with seemingly invalid hostnames, which interferes with some software.

**Describe the results you expected:**

A hostname under the `HOST_NAME_MAX` limit.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.1
API Version:  4.0.1
Go Version:   go1.17.8
Git Commit:   c8b9a2e3ec3630e9172499e15205c11b823c8107
Built:        Sun Mar 13 11:44:23 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Arch Linux -- up to date, though this also happens on Fedora CoreOS, version 36.20220325.1.0, on the "Next" stream.

comments.body:
This is very strange, I see different behavior between Podman and Buildah, and I don't beleive they are running different code.  Something strange is happening.

@flouthoc @nalind PTAL
